question: Did the model output the correct final piece sequence 3, 4, 1, 2 (representing Top-Left, Top-Right, Bottom-Left, Bottom-Right)?
Did the model explicitly and correctly map the specific images to their positions (e.g., Image 3 to Top-Left, Image 4 to Top-Right, Image 1 to Bottom-Left, Image 2 to Bottom-Right)?
Does the model's reasoning correctly identify that Image 3 (Owl head/shelves) is upside-down and requires a 180-degree rotation?
Does the model's reasoning correctly identify that Image 2 (Wolf Knight/Oxygen tank) is sideways and requires a 90-degree rotation to stand upright?
Does the model use specific visual cues (e.g., the 'EMERGENCY' sign, 'WASH HANDS' sign, or the Owl's orientation) to justify the rotation of Image 3?
Does the model justify connecting Image 3 (Top-Left) and Image 1 (Bottom-Left) based on the vertical continuity of the Owl Doctor character (head in 3, legs in 1) or the medical cabinet?
Did the model generate at least one intermediate image showing the pieces being assembled or rotated?
If an intermediate image was generated, is Image 3 depicted rotated 180 degrees (upright) compared to the input?
If an intermediate image was generated, is Image 2 depicted rotated approximately 90 degrees so the oxygen tank and Wolf Knight are upright?
In the generated images, are the seams between joined pieces (e.g., the Owl's waist or the hospital bed) aligned without obvious disjointed gaps or misaligned borders?
Do the generated images preserve the original content of the puzzle pieces without hallucinating new objects, changing characters, or severely blurring text?
In the final assembled image (or last step), does the scene exhibit correct global structure (ceiling at the top, floor at the bottom, walls vertical)?
If text appears in the generated rotated images (e.g., 'EMERGENCY' or 'WARD 7-B'), is it readable and oriented correctly?
If multiple intermediate steps were generated, do the pieces maintain consistent relative positions and visual integrity across steps (no swapping or morphing)?
If the text describes placing Image 3 in the Top-Left, does the generated image correctly show the Owl head/shelves in the top-left position?
If the text describes placing Image 4 in the Top-Right, does the generated image correctly show the Bunny/Window in the top-right position?
If the text describes placing Image 1 in the Bottom-Left, does the generated image correctly show the Medical Tray/IV/Owl legs in the bottom-left position?
If the text describes placing Image 2 in the Bottom-Right, does the generated image correctly show the Wolf Knight/Oxygen tank in the bottom-right position?
Are there any contradictions between the text description and the generated image regarding rotation (e.g., text says 'upright', image shows 'sideways')?
